channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump is set to receive the 'Patriot of the Year' award at Fox Nation event
item.description: GREENVALE, N.Y. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump, largely ensconced at Mar-a-Lago in recent weeks, will make a rare appearance outside his Florida resort to accept the “Patriot of the Year" award at Fox Nation’s Patriot Awards on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy bring Trump's DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire Elon Musk and fellow entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy spent several hours Thursday swapping ideas with lawmakers about President-elect Donald Trump'sDOGE initiative to dismantle parts of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden at tree lighting says his holiday wish is for US to continue to seek light, liberty and love
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As he presided over his final Christmas tree lighting at the White House, President Joe Biden on Thursday said his wish for the nation now, and always, was that “we continue to seek the light of liberty and love, kindness and compassion, dignity and decency.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Macron vows to stay in office till end of term, says he'll name a new prime minister soon
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron vowed Thursday to stay in office until the end of his term, due in 2027, and announced that he will name a new prime minister within days in efforts to overcome the political deadlock following the resignation of ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim Salas concedes in District 5 Board of Supervisors race
item.description: Kim Salas, the challenger to Kern County 5th District Supervisor Leticia Perez's seat, has conceded the race, falling just 133 votes short of defeating the incumbent.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Defining DOGE
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Democratic efforts to force release of Matt Gaetz ethics report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House shut down Democrats' efforts Thursday to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz, pushing the fate of any resolution to the yearslong investigation of sexual misconduct allegations into further uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth says he will be 'standing right here in this fight' after meeting with senators
item.description: Pete Hegseth met with more senators on Thursday in an effort to shore up his defense secretary nomination ahead of confirmation hearings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charlamagne tha God rips Newsom over moves to 'Trump-proof' California
item.description: Radio host Charlamagne tha God ripped California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) over his moves to “Trump-proof” California in the weeks following the president-elect's victory.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans shoot down House Dem's bid to publicize Gaetz ethics report
item.description: Democrats forced a House-wide vote on Thursday evening on whether to release an ethics report into now former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trans rights activists stage 'bathroom sit-in' near Mike Johnson's office amid Capitol Hill restroom ban
item.description: Trans rights protesters were arrested Thursday after occupying a bathroom on Capitol Hill in Washington, D.C., over a policy barring trans people from using restrooms that match their gender identity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EPA hails 'revitalized' enforcement efforts as Biden administration heads to exit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency enhanced enforcement efforts this year, doubling financial penalties issued to polluters and issuing the first-ever arrest for a climate change-related crime, the agency said in a report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Imprisoned Proud Boys leader balks at answering a prosecutor's questions about Capitol attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An imprisoned far-right extremist group leader who was the top target of the federal investigation of the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former officials urge closed-door Senate hearings on Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick for intel chief
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 100 former senior U.S. diplomats and intelligence and national security officials have urged Senate leaders to schedule closed-door hearings to allow for a full review of the government's files on former Rep.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An architect of Project 2025 is pressuring Republican senators to confirm Pete Hegseth
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The think tank behind Project 2025, the conservative blueprint linked to President-elect Donald Trump, is launching an effort to back Trump's imperiled selection for secretary of defense in its latest attempt to wield influence in the incoming Republican administration.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Louisiana Treasurer John Fleming announces bid to run for U.S. Sen. Bill Cassidy's seat
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana Treasurer John Fleming, a Republican and appointee of Donald Trump's during his first term as president, has announced that he will run for U.S. Senate in the hopes of unseating current member Bill Cassidy, also a Republican, who is up for reelection in 2026.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shouting match erupts over 9/11 during Secret Service hearing
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump dispatched his incoming envoy to the Mideast for high-level talks on reaching Gaza ceasefire
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's incoming Middle East envoy recently visited Israel and Qatar for high-level talks on landing a ceasefire and hostage deal in Gaza, a U.S. official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Senate set for more time in Washington to pass Trump agenda
item.description: The Senate is set to spend more time in Washington next year as Republicans look to expeditiously usher President-elect Trump's agenda through Congress.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: You've been elected to Congress. Now what? Freshman Republican reveals what it's like to enter office
item.description: Rep.-elect Ryan Mackenzie, R-Penn., says Republicans are poised to hit the ground running in the next Congress, unlike President-elect Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri abortion ban slated to lift Friday if judge allows
item.description: A judge must decide whether “targeted regulation of abortion providers” laws are also unconstitutional under Amendment 3.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Lisa Mascaro And Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump assassination attempt hearing devolves into screaming match
item.description: The acting director of the United States Secret Service got into a shouting match wit Rep. Pat Fallon, R-Texas, during a hearing on the two assassination attempts on President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Head of Democratic Caucus says thin House margins make attendance vital next year
item.description: The head of the House Democratic Caucus is sending an early message to his troops heading into the 119th Congress: Attendance will be crucial given the razor-tight margins in the lower chamber. “It’s important for every member to come to work and to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Concerns mount over Musk’s taste for revenge
item.description: Concerns were amplified last week after Musk went after retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman, who became an outspoken critic of President-elect Trump after testifying in his first impeachment trial.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation
